title: erythrococca oleracea
intro: Erythrococca oleracea, also known as poison goosefoot, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and respiratory issues, due to the presence of anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Erythrococca oleracea include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Erythrococca oleracea is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
